Once the Operator is installed addition: Once the Operator is installed additional configuration will be required.
Once the Operator is installed the requ: Once the Operator is installed the required custom resource will be available for creation.
**Operator backed** includes a variety o: **Operator backed** includes a variety of services managed by Kubernetes controllers.
**Operators** are software extensions to: **Operators** are software extensions to OpenShift that make use of custom resources to manage applications and their components. They are managed by Operator Lifecycle Manager.
A specific namespace on the cluster: A specific namespace on the cluster
A Subscription for this Operator already: A Subscription for this Operator already exists in Namespace "{{namespace}}"
All Operand instances for Operator <1>{{: All Operand instances for Operator <1>{{name}}</1> in <4>{{namespace}}</4> have been deleted.
Browse for a variety of managed services: Browse for a variety of managed services that are installed by cluster administrators. Cluster administrators can customize the content made available in the catalog.
Browse for Operators provided by Operato: Browse for Operators provided by Operator Lifecycle Manager.
By disabling a default source, the opera: By disabling a default source, the operators it provides will no longer appear in Software Catalog and any operator that has been installed from this source will no longer receive updates until the source is re-enabled. Disabling the source will also remove the corresponding OperatorSource and CatalogSource resources from the cluster.
Cannot create a Subscription to a non-ex: Cannot create a Subscription to a non-existent package.
Catalog will be available in all namespa: Catalog will be available in all namespaces
Catalog will only be available in a sing: Catalog will only be available in a single namespace
Catalogs are groups of Operators you can: Catalogs are groups of Operators you can make available on the cluster. Use the <2>Software Catalog</2> to subscribe and grant namespaces access to use installed Operators.
Cluster in Azure Workload Identity / Fed: Cluster in Azure Workload Identity / Federated Identity Mode
Cluster in GCP Workload Identity / Feder: Cluster in GCP Workload Identity / Federated Identity Mode

Denotes whether this CatalogSource provi: Denotes whether this CatalogSource provides operators to a specific namespace, or the entire cluster.

Each Namespace can subscribe to a single: Each Namespace can subscribe to a single channel of a package for automatic updates.

If your Operator configured off-cluster: If your Operator configured off-cluster resources, these will continue to run and require manual cleanup.
Inspect the requirements for the compone: Inspect the requirements for the components specified in this InstallPlan before approving.
Install your Operator by subscribing to: Install your Operator by subscribing to one of the update channels to keep the Operator up to date. The strategy determines either manual or automatic updates.
Installation namespace <1>{{namespace}}<: Installation namespace <1>{{namespace}}</1> contains <4></4> installed with manual approval, and all operators installed in the same namespace will function as manual approval strategy. To allow automatic approval, all operators installed in the namespace must use automatic approval strategy.
Installed Operators are represented by C: Installed Operators are represented by ClusterServiceVersions within this Namespace.
Installing an operator with manual appro: Installing an operator with manual approval causes all operators installed in namespace <1>{{selectedTargetNamespace}}</1> to function as manual approval strategy and will be updated altogether. Install operators into separate namespaces for handling their updates independently. To allow automatic approval, all operators installed in the namespace must use automatic approval strategy.
Installing this Operator in the selected: Installing this Operator in the selected Namespace would cause conflicts with another Operator providing these APIs:
InstallPlans are created automatically b: InstallPlans are created automatically by subscriptions or manually using the CLI.
Learn more about Red Hat’s third party s: Learn more about Red Hat’s third party software support policy
Manual approval applies to all operators: Manual approval applies to all operators in a namespace
Manual update approval is required when: Manual update approval is required when not installing the latest version for the selected channel.
Missing sufficient privileges for manual: Missing sufficient privileges for manual InstallPlan approval

Operators provided by this source will n: Operators provided by this source will not appear in Software Catalog and any operators installed from this source will not receive updates until this source is re-enabled.
Please note that installing non-Red Hat: Please note that installing non-Red Hat operators into OpenShift namespaces and enabling monitoring voids user support. Enabling cluster monitoring for non-Red Hat operators can lead to malicious metrics data overriding existing cluster metrics.

Preferred rules specify that, if the rul: Preferred rules specify that, if the rule is met, the scheduler tries to enforce the rules, but does not guarantee enforcement.

Review the manual install plan for opera: Review the manual install plan for operators <1></1>. Once approved, the following resources will be created in order to satisfy the requirements for the components specified in the plan. Click the resource name to view the resource in detail.
Select the checkbox below to also remove: Select the checkbox below to also remove all Operands associated with this Operator.
Some fields might not be displayed in th: Some fields might not be displayed in this form. Select "YAML view" to edit all fields.
The {{namespace}} Namespace is reserved: The {{namespace}} Namespace is reserved for global Operators that watch all Namespaces. To install an Operator in a single Namespace, select a different Namespace where the operand should run.

The operator did not install successfull: The operator did not install successfully.
The Operator does not support to be made: The Operator does not support to be made available in a single namespace (OwnNamespace installMode) or global installation (AllNamespaces installMode).  Use the CLI to install this Operator instead.

This cluster is using AWS Security Token: This cluster is using AWS Security Token Service to reach the cloud API. In order for this operator to take the actions it requires directly with the cloud API, you must provide a role ARN (with an attached policy) during installation. Please see the operator description for more details.

This cluster is using AWS Security Token Service to reach the cloud API. In order for this operator to take the actions it requires directly with the cloud API, you will need to provide a role ARN (with an attached policy) during installation. Manual subscriptions are highly recommended as steps should be taken prior to upgrade to ensure that the permissions required by the next version are properly accounted for in the role. Please see the operator description for more details.
This cluster is using Azure Workload Ide: This cluster is using Azure Workload Identity / Federated Identity to reach the cloud API. In order for this operator to take the actions it requires directly with the cloud API, provide the Client ID, Tenant ID, and Subscription ID during installation. Manual subscriptions are highly recommended as steps should be taken before upgrade to ensure that the permissions required by the next version are properly accounted for in the role. See the operator description for more details.
